title: Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary
tagline: Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Bihar

overview.short: Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary is located in Bhagalpur District of Bihar, India.
overview.description: Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary is located in Bhagalpur District of Bihar, India. The sanctuary is a 60 kilometers stretch of the Ganges River from Sultanganj to Kahalgaon in Bhagalpur district. notified as Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary in 1991, it is the protected area for the endangered Gangetic dolphins in Asia. Once found in abundance, only a few hundred remain, of which half are found here.

topPlaces.name: Government Polytechnic, Bhagalpur
topPlaces.description: Government Polytechnic, Bhagalpur is a polytechnic institute in Bihar, India. It is administered by Department of Science and Technology, Bihar and is funded by Government of Bihar.

topPlaces.name: Bhagalpur division
topPlaces.description: Bhagalpur division is one of the nine administrative units of Bihar, a state in the east of India. Bhagalpur city is the headquarters of the division, which was established in 1829 and is the oldest division of the state.

topPlaces.name: Sabour, Bhagalpur
topPlaces.description: Sabour is an adjoining town of Bhagalpur city and headquarter of Sabour block in Bhagalpur district in the Indian state of Bihar.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Bhagalpur (1745)
topPlaces.description: Battle of Bhagalpur in 1745 was a military conflict between Marathas under Raghuji I of Nagpur and Nawab Alivardi Khan in the Bihar province of Bengal Subah.

topPlaces.name: Statue of Vasupujya
topPlaces.description: The Statue of Vasupujya located at Champapur,Bhagalpur in the Indian state of Bihar, is one of the tallest statues in eastern India and the tallest statue of Lord Vasupujya in India. The statue is dedicated to Vasupujya, the twelfth Jain Tirthankara of the present cosmic age.

topPlaces.name: Bhagalpur
topPlaces.description: Bhagalpur (official and romanised name), historically known as Champa Nagari, is a city in the Indian state of Bihar, situated on the southern bank of the Ganges river. It is the third largest city of Bihar by population and also serves the headquarters of Bhagalpur district, Bhagalpur division, and Eastern Range.

topPlaces.name: Nehru Smarak Stadium
topPlaces.description: Nehru Smarak Stadium is a cricket ground Bhagalpur, Bihar. The ground hosted two Ranji matches for the Bihar cricket team in 1972 and 1973 where Odisha cricket team and Assam cricket team were the visiting teams.

topPlaces.name: Sandish Compound
topPlaces.description: Sandy's Compound is a 30-year-old sports complex located in, Bhagalpur City, Bihar, opposite of police line Bhagalpur. It contains a Doordarshan TV Centre, volleyball court and a small open stadium.

items.note: Government Polytechnic, Bhagalpur is a polytechnic institute in Bihar, India. It is administered by Department of Science and Technology, Bihar and is funded by Government of Bihar.

Bhagalpur division is one of the nine administrative units of Bihar, a state in the east of India. Bhagalpur city is the headquarters of the division, which was established in 1829 and is the oldest division of the state.
Sabour is an adjoining town of Bhagalpur city and headquarter of Sabour block in Bhagalpur district in the Indian state of Bihar.
Battle of Bhagalpur in 1745 was a military conflict between Marathas under Raghuji I of Nagpur and Nawab Alivardi Khan in the Bihar province of Bengal Subah.

The Statue of Vasupujya located at Champapur,Bhagalpur in the Indian state of Bihar, is one of the tallest statues in eastern India and the tallest statue of Lord Vasupujya in India. The statue is dedicated to Vasupujya, the twelfth Jain Tirthankara of the present cosmic age.

Bhagalpur (official and romanised name), historically known as Champa Nagari, is a city in the Indian state of Bihar, situated on the southern bank of the Ganges river. It is the third largest city of Bihar by population and also serves the headquarters of Bhagalpur district, Bhagalpur division, and Eastern Range.

Nehru Smarak Stadium is a cricket ground Bhagalpur, Bihar. The ground hosted two Ranji matches for the Bihar cricket team in 1972 and 1973 where Odisha cricket team and Assam cricket team were the visiting teams.
Sandy's Compound is a 30-year-old sports complex located in, Bhagalpur City, Bihar, opposite of police line Bhagalpur. It contains a Doordarshan TV Centre, volleyball court and a small open stadium.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (198 km) and the nearest railway station is Katihar Junction (63 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Government Polytechnic, Bhagalpur, Bhagalpur division, Sabour, Bhagalpur, Battle of Bhagalpur (1745), Statue of Vasupujya.
